symptoms.symptom_name: Acute solid-food bolus impaction, mid-meal onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A solid-food bolus lodging mid-meal is the defining presentation of mechanical oesophageal impaction.
A clear mechanical swallowing trigger lowers the probability that the pain is cardiac — but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to swallow saliva with hypersalivation/drooling

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to handle secretions marks complete obstruction and mandates urgent (emergent) endoscopic disimpaction with aspiration precautions.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Retrosternal pressure at the level of the impacted bolus and oesophageal spasm.
Retrosternal pain in an adult mandates active exclusion of a cardiac cause; ECG/troponin required and unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive intermittent dysphagia to dry solids over 6 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic progressive dysphagia to dry solids is the classic fibrostenotic EoE trajectory that culminates in impaction.
Also causes progressive dysphagia and food hold-up — distinguished by manometry/endoscopy.
A reflux-related stricture can produce solid-food dysphagia; less likely without a prominent reflux history.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Atopic terrain substantially raises the pre-test probability of EoE as the underlying disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: No exertional trigger, no radiation to arm/jaw, no diaphoresis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of exertional onset, radiation and diaphoresis lowers ACS probability but does not exclude it without objective testing.

symptoms.symptom_name: No haematemesis, no sudden tearing pain, no pleuritic pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of tearing pain, haematemesis and pleuritic pain lowers the probability of perforation — but it remains a complication to safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: No stridor, no dyspnoea, normal voice — airway patent

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the obstruction is oesophageal rather than an airway foreign body; airway currently safe though aspiration risk persists.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG and serum troponin (to exclude ACS)

investigation_details.expected_result: No acute ischaemic ECG changes and a normal troponin would be expected given the mechanical trigger — but this cannot be assumed and is required to exclude ACS; not obtainable via telehealth.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A cardiac cause of the retrosternal pain is not excluded and an evolving ACS could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are the objective tests needed to safely exclude a cardiac cause of retrosternal pain.

symptoms.symptom_name: Gastroscopy: impacted bolus with oesophageal rings/furrows/exudates; biopsy showing eosinophilic infiltration

investigation_details.expected_result: Impacted food bolus with endoscopic features of EoE (fixed rings, longitudinal furrows, white exudates, narrow-calibre oesophagus); biopsies show eosinophil-predominant inflammation (≥15 eosinophils/hpf).
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The bolus is not relieved and the underlying EoE is never confirmed or treated.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Endoscopic disimpaction plus biopsy-confirmed oesophageal eosinophilia is the diagnostic gold standard for EoE — sealed, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals: mild sinus tachycardia and mildly elevated blood pressure (distress-related)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia/hypertension reflects pain and distress; non-specific and clinician-measured, so sealed and not available to the AI Doctor.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute solid-food impaction + a six-month history of progressive dry-solid dysphagia + atopic background together point strongly to fibrostenotic EoE as the substrate, above an isolated one-off choke.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Oesophageal food bolus impaction on eosinophilic oesophagitis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: A lodged bolus plus inability to swallow saliva defines complete obstruction — the feature that converts this from an urgent-outpatient problem into a same-day ED / emergent-endoscopy problem with aspiration risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Retrosternal chest pain in an adult — an ECG is mandatory to exclude a cardiac cause before attributing the pain to the oesophagus; cannot be done via telehealth, so it drives in-person ED assessment.
Complements the ECG to exclude ACS; a single value may be insufficient early, reinforcing the need for in-person assessment.
Definitive endoscopic disimpaction of the bolus and biopsies to confirm EoE at the index procedure — emergent if the patient cannot manage secretions.
Reserved for suspected perforation (no perforation features here); would be arranged in ED if red flags evolve.
Contraindicated in acute complete impaction and suspected perforation — barium risks aspiration and contaminates the mediastinum if perforated; do not order acutely.
